name: Howard County Education
description_short: Howard County Education Federal Credit Union is a Maryland-based credit union established in 1953, serving over 4,000 members primarily in the education sector with $26M in assets.
description_long:
Howard County Education Federal Credit Union was established in 1953 and is headquartered in Ellicott City, Maryland. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative designed to serve employees and affiliates of educational organizations in the Howard County area. The institution has grown to serve over 4,000 loyal members and maintains $26 million in assets under management.

HCEFCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services typical of community credit unions, including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRA/KEOGH accounts, and mortgage loans. Members can access bill pay, e-statements, remote deposit capture, and financial calculators. The credit union emphasizes member education through financial literacy resources and offers student scholarships to support the education community it serves. Online and mobile banking platforms are available for iOS and Android devices.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on the education sector, long operational history (70+ years), and emphasis on member relationships. With only 1 branch and no ATMs, HCEFCU operates as a lean, locally-focused institution rather than a large regional network. The credit union promotes competitive advantages through lower fees compared to traditional banks and maintains a reputation for knowledgeable, helpful staff according to member reviews.

As a small credit union with limited physical infrastructure (1 branch, 0 ATMs), accessibility may be constrained for members outside the Ellicott City area. The organization is appropriate for education sector employees and affiliated organizations seeking community-focused banking, but prospective members should verify membership eligibility requirements. Recent online security concerns noted in member reviews regarding expired SSL certificates warrant verification of current security status before conducting sensitive transactions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 1,300+ accounts)
Mortgage loans (home purchase, refinance, and other options)
Mobile banking app for iOS and Android
pros: Established 70+ years ago (since 1953) with consistent member satisfaction
Specialized focus on education sector with tailored financial solutions
Membership of 4,000+ with $26M in assets indicates stability and member trust
Offers comprehensive services including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and CDs
Provides financial education resources and student scholarships to members
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android devices
Member reports indicate knowledgeable, helpful staff with long-term reliability
cons: Only 1 branch location in Ellicott City with 0 ATMs limits accessibility for dispersed members
Recent member reports of expired SSL certificate on website raising security concerns
Membership restricted to educational organizations/employees, not open to general public
Very small institution (6 employees) may limit service capacity and product availability compared to larger credit unions
best_for: Education sector employees (teachers, administrators, school staff) in Howard County, Maryland
Affiliates of educational organizations seeking community-focused banking relationships
Members prioritizing personal service and local decision-making over branch convenience
Individuals seeking lower fees and member-owned financial institution structure
diagnosis: Best for education sector employees and affiliated organization members in the Ellicott City, Maryland area seeking personalized service and lower fees from a community credit union. Primary caveat: membership eligibility is restricted to educational organizations, physical accessibility is limited to a single branch with no ATM network, and recent website security issues should be verified before account access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
